cards.title: Whispered wisdom of a parent's last words
cards.body: Dr Ijaz Durrani celebrates the precious nature of parting words from dying parents to children. Polly Hudson's moving reflection explores how deathbed advice shapes a child's life.

cards.title: The pet that taught us how to raise a family
cards.body: A family discovers that raising a pet rabbit proves harder than raising their babies. The experience teaches unexpected lessons about nurturing a creature you can't reason with.

cards.title: Living by advice dad gave on his deathbed
cards.body: Polly Hudson reflects on how her father's deathbed advice continues to guide her 14 years later. She traces the ways she's become like her imperfect dad through his final words.

cards.title: Keeping dead wife's books safe for our son
cards.body: Ben O'Mara finds healing by preserving his late wife's favorite novels for their son. Reading together helps him release guilt and reconnect with the world his wife shared with him.

cards.title: The impotence of parenting a teenager doing A-levels
cards.body: Zoe Williams explores the helplessness parents feel when supporting teenagers through advanced exams. Holding flashcards for complex subjects leaves parents with unique anxiety and uncertainty.

cards.title: The value of humility when parenting adolescents
cards.body: Parents who maintain modest expectations about their influence on adolescent growth reduce pressure on themselves. This approach fosters healthier relationships during the teenage years.

cards.title: The forgotten years of middle childhood
cards.body: Middle childhood is often overlooked but profoundly reshapes children, parents, and their connection. This developmental period deserves more attention from parents and researchers alike.

cards.title: Play and creativity run on free energy
cards.body: Children's play and creativity thrive when free from rigid structure and competition. These moments balance surprise with caution, enabling natural development.

cards.title: Child mental healthcare not built for children of color
cards.body: Child psychiatry was not designed to protect children of color and carries racist origins. Parents of color should understand this history before pursuing mental healthcare for their children.

cards.title: Parent guilt: letting go and learning lessons
cards.body: Parents often dwell on past mistakes, but these errors teach valuable present-day lessons. Releasing guilt helps parents focus on current growth and relationship-building with their children.

cards.title: What families can do after dementia diagnosis
cards.body: A dementia diagnosis transforms more than memory—it reshapes the entire family dynamic. Families can plan proactively for care, protect connection, and support the caregiver before crisis hits.

cards.title: My daughter woke with numb arm and died weeks later
cards.body: A mother shares her daughter's tragic story after a brain tumour diagnosis. She's determined her daughter's death must drive awareness and change.
